expressions.english: to give a punishment; to punish
expressions.notes: 벌 = punishment

expressions.english: to give a prize
expressions.notes: 상 = prize; award

expressions.english: to give as a present
expressions.notes: 선물 = gift, present

expressions.english: to give something to eat; to feed
expressions.notes:
먹을 것 = food, something to eat
먹을 것 literally means "thing to eat," but it is a set expression that refers to food in general.

expressions.english: Please give me more.
expressions.notes:
더 = more
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.english: Please give me (some), too.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
-도 = too, also

expressions.english: Give me some hints.
expressions.notes:
힌트 = hint; heads-up
좀 = a little; please

expressions.english: Please give me that book.
expressions.notes: 그 = the; that

expressions.english: Please give me a little more time.
expressions.notes: 시간 = time; hour

expressions.english:
Did you give water to the flowers yesterday?
Did you water the flowers yesterday?
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
꽃 = flower
물 = water

expressions.english:
Give me the same one.
I will have the same one.
expressions.notes:
같다 = to be the same
같은 is the adjective form of 같다.

expressions.english: I heard if you buy it now, they will give you one more.
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
사다 = to buy
-(으)면 = if/when + S + V, once + S + V
하나 = one (native Korean number)
-대요 = I heard that + S + V. They say + S + V

expressions.korean: 엄마, 책 사거 돈 좀 주세요.
expressions.english: Mom, please give me some money so that I can buy books.
expressions.notes:
엄마 = mom
-게 = so that + S can + V
돈 = money

expressions.english: My friend gave me that as a present.
expressions.notes:
그거 = that
친구 = friend

expressions.english: You didn't give me that yesterday.
expressions.notes: 안 = not

expressions.korean: 이거 제가 작년에 준 장갑 아니에요?
expressions.english: Aren't these the gloves I gave you last year?
expressions.notes:
이거 = this thing; this
제 = (polite) my; I
지난 = last year
장갑 = glove
아니다 = to be not

expressions.korean: 목이 너무 말라서 그러는데 물 좀 주세
expressions.english: I'm thirsty, so please give me some water.
expressions.notes:
목 = throat; neck
너무 = too much, excessively; very
목이 마르다 = to be thirsty

expressions.korean: 작년에 제가 준 책 아직도 다 안 읽었어요?
expressions.english: You still have not finished the book I gave you last year?
expressions.notes:
아직도 = still, (not) yet
읽다 = to read

expressions.korean: 친구가 콘서트 티켓을 줘서 어제 보고 왔어요.
expressions.english: My friend gave me concert tickets, so I went and watched it yesterday.
expressions.notes:
콘서트 = concert
티켓 = ticket
-아이/어서 = because, since
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
-고 오다 = to go do something and then come back

expressions.korean: 친구한테 선물로 주려고 샀는데 너무 마음에 들어서 그냥 제가 쓸 거예요.
expressions.english: I bought it to give it to my friend as a present, but I like it so much that I will just use it myself.
expressions.notes:
-(으)려고 = in order to + V
마음에 들다 = to like
그냥 = just, only, simply
쓰다 = to use; to spend (+ money)
-(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; be going to + V
